transcription.text: In this New York apartment building overlooking Central Park in the lights of the city, a well-known caterer is preparing an after-show party for the cast of Louis the Louse. Right, Sid? They'll all be here, the whole cast, thirsty and hungry like all actors after a show. So we have plenty of food ready, beer, and lots of calling redcap ale on ice. You know, redcap's the favorite ale at most parties I cater. Why? Because people who really know good ale go for the heartier flavor of redcap. It's got what I call heft, a taste you remember. And here, you see that rich, deep amber color? Let's you know that redcap is special, hearty enough to satisfy the appetites of the show crowd. Yes, redcap's got heft. It's all ale, and it does the greatest disappearing act in show business. Enjoy, Captain. If you're calling redcap ale yourself, it's got heft.

metadata.title: Carling's Brewery Red Cap Ale Beer
